findings.description: Off-by-one in assemble: `range(session.total_chunks - 1)` concatenates all chunks except the last, so every assembled file is silently truncated (and total_chunks==1 yields an empty file), despite is_complete having verified all chunks arrived.

assemble writes the file to the permanent UPLOAD_DIR and deletes the session (line 70) BEFORE any integrity check; verify_checksum (80) and file_size_ok (90) are separate entry points the caller must remember to invoke, with no rollback, so a corrupt/oversized file is already committed by the time validation could reject it.

MAX_FILE_MB is never enforced on the write path: receive_chunk (42) imposes no per-chunk size cap and assemble (58) streams chunks to disk unconditionally, so file_size_ok can only flag an oversized file after it has already been fully written, allowing disk exhaustion regardless of the limit.

dest_path is built from the client-supplied filename with no collision handling, so two uploads sharing a filename silently overwrite each other in UPLOAD_DIR (and an absolute or '../' filename makes os.path.join discard UPLOAD_DIR entirely — also a path-traversal security issue, deferred to critic-security).

File I/O in receive_chunk/assemble/verify_checksum has no error handling, so OSError/IOError (disk full, permission denied, missing chunk file) propagates raw; this is an inconsistent contract for the `-> bool` methods, which return False for logical rejection but throw on I/O failure instead of degrading gracefully.

Session/chunk lifecycle leaks resources: if start succeeds and chunks are received but neither assemble nor abort is ever called, the in-memory session and the on-disk chunk files persist forever with no TTL/expiry or reaping mechanism.

start() overwrites an existing in-flight session for the same upload_id without rejecting or cleaning up its already-written chunk files, orphaning the prior session's chunks on disk and resetting its received set.

verify_checksum does f.read() of the entire file into memory (up to the 200 MB allowed size) instead of streaming/hashing in blocks, defeating the memory-efficiency purpose of chunked uploads — deferred to critic-performance.

If assemble is invoked twice for the same upload_id (e.g. concurrent/retried calls), the second `del self._sessions[upload_id]` raises KeyError because the guard at line 61 and the delete are not atomic; minor given no concurrency is declared in scope.
Checksum comparison uses non-constant-time `!=` on the hex digest rather than hmac.compare_digest; minor timing-side-channel note, deferred to critic-security.
